content_markdown: A pipe, open at both ends, has a length $L$. The speed of sound in the air in the pipe is $v$. The frequency of vibration of the fundamental (first harmonic) standing wave that can be set up in the pipe is

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: For a pipe open at both ends, the fundamental frequency occurs when the length of the pipe $L$ is equal to half a wavelength ($\lambda/2$). Since $v = f\lambda$, we can substitute $\lambda = 2L$ into the wave equation to get $v = f(2L)$, which rearranges to $f = \frac{v}{2L}$.
